splits.title: The Vision
splits.author: Elsie C. Carroll
splits.start_boundary: The Vision. Elsie C. Carroll. The moon peeped in
splits.end_boundary: My boy's a savior on a cross.

files.notes: The file contains a long article about Native Americans followed by a poem about a mother's prayer for her soldier son. The article includes the '(To be continued.)' notation at the end, indicating it continues in a subsequent issue. The poem has clear visual separation and author attribution.

splits.title: Guide Lessons
splits.start_boundary: or person at whose instance the Temple work
splits.end_boundary: This is a great favorite in President Smith's

files.notes: This file contains only the Guide Lessons section from the November 1918 issue. It includes four lessons: Theology and Testimony (Book of Mormon lesson on abolishing kings), Work and Business (second week), Genealogy (patronymics and temple work), and Home Courses (meat substitutes). The file appears to be a continuation from a previous section, starting mid-sentence about temple work identification requirements.
